message:
feat(mail-ui): per-recipient read tracking, importance filter propagation, URL encoding, and live mark-read

Unified Inbox per-recipient read state:
- Add recipient_read (BTreeMap<String, bool>) to UnifiedMessageAggregate
  and UnifiedMessage, tracking read state per individual recipient rather
  than only the aggregate all_read boolean
- Expose recipient_names (Vec<String>) alongside the comma-joined
  recipients string for structured client-side access
- Wire recipient_read through the API JSON payload and Alpine.js state
- Add isMessageRead() and recomputeAggregateRead() JS helpers that
  respect the active recipient filter: when filtering by a specific
  recipient, read state reflects that recipient's inbox, not the
  aggregate

Importance filter propagation:
- HTML route and API route now both clamp limit to 1..=1000 (was 10000
  for HTML, 50000 for API) keeping them aligned so live refreshes never
  shrink the rendered dataset
- When a server-side importance filter is active, over-fetch the
  per-agent inbox (up to 10,000 rows) before aggregation so filtered
  high-priority messages behind newer normal-priority rows remain visible
- Pass filter_importance from API query string through to the aggregation
  and back to the Alpine.js initial state (initialImportanceFilter)
- Importance filter changes trigger a server re-fetch (silent) to get
  correctly filtered data, with clearFilters() also re-fetching if
  importance was previously set

URL encoding for thread IDs:
- Add percent_decode_path_segment() (preserves literal '+') and
  percent_encode_path_segment() for proper RFC 3986 path encoding
- Add mail_thread_href() helper producing pre-encoded thread URLs
- Thread route now decodes the path segment before DB lookup instead of
  using the raw URL fragment, fixing threads with '/', ' ', or '+' chars
- All templates (inbox, message, search, unified) now use
  encodeURIComponent() or the server-encoded thread_url instead of raw
  interpolation

Mark-read overhaul:
- markSelectedAsRead() is now async, issuing real POST requests to
  /mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-read grouped by (project, agent)
  with 500-ID batches and Promise.allSettled
- Client-side state is optimistically updated from successful responses:
  recipient_read entries are flipped to true, aggregate read is
  recomputed, and the selection list is pruned for fully-marked messages
- handle_mark_read server handler deduplicates input IDs, distinguishes
  newly-marked vs. already-read counts using timestamp comparison, and
  returns already_read_count + unique_requested_count in the response

Filter UX refinements:
- filterMessages() now prunes selectedMessages to visible IDs so batch
  actions cannot operate on hidden rows after a filter change
- Split view auto-selects the first message when the current selection
  is filtered away
- uniqueRecipients and recipient filter now prefer the structured
  recipient_names array over comma-splitting the display string

Tests:
- UnifiedMessageAggregate into_view surfaces recipient_names/read
- unified_api_message_value includes recipient_names/read
- render_unified_inbox serializes normalized importance filter
- render_unified_inbox wires importance filter refresh handler
- importance filter over-fetches past newer non-matching rows
- API unified inbox preserves importance filter
- API unified inbox includes thread references for root messages
- thread route decodes percent-encoded thread IDs
- percent_decode_path_segment keeps '+' literal
- percent_encode_path_segment escapes reserved bytes
- mail_thread_href encodes thread path segment
- handle_mark_read deduplicates IDs and excludes already-read from count

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
